report.summary: OpenClaw Dashboard has a moderate security posture. The application has no hardcoded secrets and uses environment variables correctly, but lacks critical security headers, input validation, and authentication mechanisms.

sensitiveInformationLeakage.notes: No hardcoded secrets found. Environment variables properly used. .gitignore file is missing.

networkSecurity.notes: CORS is overly permissive. No CSP headers. No HTTPS enforcement. WebSocket uses unencrypted ws://.

dependencySecurity.notes: No known vulnerabilities in npm audit. Several outdated dependencies.

dataSecurity.notes: No input validation. Error details exposed to clients. No rate limiting.

high.title: Missing .gitignore file
high.description: No .gitignore file exists, risking accidental commit of sensitive files (.env, credentials, session data).
high.impact: Sensitive configuration or credential files could be committed to version control.
high.recommendation: Create a comprehensive .gitignore file including: .env, .env.*, *.log, node_modules, dist, coverage, .DS_Store, session data directories.

high.title: Overly Permissive CORS Configuration
high.description: CORS is configured with app.use(cors()) without any restrictions, allowing requests from any origin.
high.impact: Any malicious website can make API requests to this server, enabling CSRF-like attacks.
high.recommendation: Restrict CORS to specific allowed origins: app.use(cors({ origin: ['http://localhost:3777', 'https://yourdomain.com'] }))

high.title: No Authentication on API Endpoints
high.description: All API endpoints are publicly accessible without any authentication or authorization.
high.impact: Anyone can access session data, system metrics, and other sensitive information.
high.recommendation: Implement authentication middleware (JWT, API key, or session-based auth) for protected endpoints.

high.title: Missing Security Headers
high.description: No security headers (CSP, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, HSTS) are set.
high.impact: Vulnerable to XSS, clickjacking, and other client-side attacks.
high.recommendation: Install and configure helmet.js: const helmet = require('helmet'); app.use(helmet());

medium.title: No Input Validation on Query Parameters
medium.description: Query parameters (page, limit, search, status) are used without validation or sanitization.
medium.impact: Potential for injection attacks or unexpected behavior with malformed input.
medium.recommendation: Add input validation using joi, express-validator, or manual checks. Validate types, ranges, and patterns.

medium.title: Error Details Exposed to Client
medium.description: ErrorBoundary component displays error stack traces and component stacks to users.
medium.impact: Reveals internal application structure and potentially sensitive information.
medium.recommendation: Only show generic error messages to users. Log detailed errors server-side only.

medium.title: No Rate Limiting
medium.description: No rate limiting middleware is applied to API endpoints.
medium.impact: Vulnerable to DoS attacks and API abuse.
medium.recommendation: Implement rate limiting using express-rate-limit middleware.

medium.title: WebSocket Uses Unencrypted Protocol
medium.description: WebSocket connection uses ws:// instead of wss://.
medium.impact: Real-time data transmitted in plaintext, vulnerable to interception.
medium.recommendation: Use wss:// protocol in production and implement WebSocket authentication.

medium.title: File System Access Without Path Validation
medium.description: Server reads files from hardcoded paths without validation.
medium.impact: Potential path traversal if user input influences file paths.
medium.recommendation: Validate and sanitize all file paths. Use path.resolve() and ensure paths are within expected directories.

medium.title: Missing Content-Security-Policy Meta Tag
medium.description: HTML head lacks CSP meta tag for additional client-side protection.
medium.recommendation: Add CSP meta tag: <meta http-equiv="Content-Security-Policy" content="default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'">

low.title: Console Logging in Production
low.description: Multiple console.log/error statements throughout the codebase.
low.impact: Potential information leakage in production logs.
low.recommendation: Use a proper logging library (winston, pino) with log levels. Disable debug logs in production.

low.title: Deprecated WebSocket Path Configuration
low.description: WebSocketServer configured with deprecated 'path' option in constructor.
low.impact: May cause compatibility issues with future ws library versions.
low.recommendation: Move path configuration to the handleUpgrade method or use noServer option.

low.title: Outdated Dependencies
low.description: Several dependencies have newer major versions available.
low.impact: May miss security patches and bug fixes in newer versions.
low.recommendation: Update to latest stable versions: react 19, react-router-dom 7, framer-motion 12, recharts 3.

low.title: No HTTPS Configuration
low.description: Server only supports HTTP, no HTTPS/TLS configuration provided.
low.recommendation: Use a reverse proxy (nginx) for TLS termination or configure HTTPS directly with ssl certificates.

low.title: Docker Container Port Exposure
low.description: Docker compose exposes port 3777 directly to host.
low.impact: Service directly accessible from host network.
low.recommendation: Bind to localhost only: '127.0.0.1:3777:3777' if not using reverse proxy.

positiveFindings: No hardcoded API keys or secrets in source code
Environment variables used for configuration (PORT, USE_REAL_DATA, NODE_ENV)
Docker container runs as non-root user (nodejs:1001)
No known vulnerabilities in npm dependencies
Source maps disabled in production build
Production dependencies properly separated from devDependencies
No dangerouslySetInnerHTML or eval() usage in frontend

immediate: Create .gitignore file to prevent accidental credential commits
Install and configure helmet.js for security headers
Restrict CORS to specific allowed origins
Add input validation middleware to all API endpoints
Implement basic authentication for API access
shortTerm: Remove error stack traces from client-facing responses
Implement proper logging with log levels
Add HTTPS support or configure reverse proxy
longTerm: Set up security monitoring and alerting
Implement API versioning with deprecation policies
